masked_case_json.world_change: a pre-revenue satellite communications company depended on scarce third-party launch capacity to execute an aggressive constellation deployment plan. a catastrophic launch-provider test explosion introduced uncertain return-to-flight timing and materially increased deployment-delay risk, directly weakening the near-term capacity and cash-flow path.

resolved_outcome_json.external_mechanism_check: Underlying: AST SpaceMobile and major launch-provider incident period (late May 2026). Primary sources: launch-provider incident disclosures and company-facing deployment dependency context. Outcome check: catastrophic provider failure with uncertain return-to-flight timing increased schedule risk and weakened near-term deployment confidence; a disciplined model should not open a long on deployment optimism immediately after this event. Do not expose this field to the model during decision generation.
